text: Abstract Bicycloalkanes, cubanes and their structural analogues have become over the last decade of research a widely recognized class of bioisoster of (hetero)arenes. Consequently, numerous new strategies have been developed to address the growing demand for these molecules. In this review we report on the progress achieved in the area of photochemical methodologies for the synthesis and modification of these bioisosteric molecules. We focus on the synthesis of various structural motifs and on the mechanisms of the different photochemical reactions leading to the formation of: (i) [1.1.1]‐bicyclopentanes (BCPs); (ii) [2.2.1]‐bicyclohexanes (BCHs); (iii) [3.1.1]‐bicycloheptanes (BCHeps); and (iv) cubanes as well as related structures. Finally, we discuss the perspective of the field towards the discovery of new photochemical reactions and their applications to the synthesis of novel and highly functionalized bioisoster molecules.
